ai_analysis.one_sentence_summary: A fixable CVSS 9.8 zlib flaw in redis-cart is a patch-and-redeploy priority before memory-safety abuse could take down your shopping cache.
ai_analysis.true_positive_rationale: ACS names CVE-2026-22184 with an exact component version and a newer fixed zlib build; that is a scanner-grounded finding, not a heuristic guess.
ai_analysis.attack_vector: Anyone who can reach redis-cart or influence its workload could potentially trigger zlib parsing paths for denial-of-service or memory corruption, starving carts or corrupting session data.
ai_analysis.blast_radius: Single Deployment (redis-cart) in ms-demo; blast radius grows if every checkout path depends on this cache being healthy.
ai_analysis.prioritization_rationale: Critical CVSS plus a published fix path keeps this above routine backlog work, tempered slightly because exposure depends on how redis is reachable from the mesh.
recommended_actions: Rebuild or rebase the redis image so zlib is at least 1.3.2-r0, then roll out redis-cart.
Confirm the violation clears in ACS after deploy and promote the image only from CI that re-scans.
Restrict redis connectivity with NetworkPolicy if it should never see north-south traffic.
mitigation_strategies: Until the image ships, block unintended admin or debug access to the redis service from inside the cluster.
